replay_env: GE007_DISABLE_LEVEL_INTRO=1 GE007_AUTO_ADD_ITEM=8 GE007_AUTO_ADD_ITEM_FRAME=30 GE007_AUTO_ADD_WEAPON_AMMO=8 GE007_AUTO_ADD_WEAPON_AMMO_AMOUNT=600 GE007_AUTO_ADD_WEAPON_AMMO_FRAME=30 GE007_AUTO_EQUIP_ITEM=8 GE007_AUTO_EQUIP_ITEM_FRAME=45
note: AK47 (ITEM_AK47=8, full-auto machinegun) sustained-fire tape on the Dam (FID-0056 / P1d). Bond is given+equipped the AK47 with 600 rifle rounds (replay_env, deterministic state injection) and holds fire for 400 ticks (GE007_AUTO_FIRE=80:400 baked into the captured OSContPad stream). Unlike the PP7 tape (dam_combat_guard6) this reaches the full-auto machinegun case in src/game/gun.c (~line 18359-18372: fire_rate = bondwalkItemGetAutomaticFiringRate() then fireRateEffectiveAutoRate()), so it is the end-to-end gate for the FID-0056 fire-rate call-site that the ROM-free unit test (tests/test_fire_rate_authentic.c) cannot cover (P1d: the unit test re-implements the gate; a call-site revert stays green). sim_state_hash is the DEFAULT (Input.FireRateAuthentic=1, faithful N64 cadence) hash; fire_rate_authentic_off_hash is the pre-FID-0056 legacy-cadence hash (GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0), which is exactly what a gun.c call-site revert reproduces. ON != OFF proves the tape exercises the divisor: a call-site revert reddens this baseline. Deterministic: record==replay1==replay2==replay3 (byte-exact). Regenerate only via scratchpad/record_ak47_final.sh (canonical recipe; keep in sync with tools/fidelity/ak47_fire_rate_capture_smoke.sh). Guarded by tools/fidelity/ak47_fire_rate_capture_smoke.sh (ctest port_ak47_fire_rate_capture_smoke) + tools/fidelity/tape_regression.sh (via replay_env). Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (857329bd0ebe6c07→e228f3a765adc3eb): this Dam tape's window also holds a guard (chr=6, KF7/item=8, AutomaticFiringRate=3) in sustained line-of-sight full-auto at Bond; the recorded 857329bd0ebe6c07 was captured (commit 9e661b3) against a stale pre-FID-0066 ge007 binary (linked before the FID-0066 chrlv.c fix landed, despite the source tree already containing it), so it reflects a mixed state -- player fire-rate scaled (FID-0056), guard fire-rate NOT yet scaled (pre-FID-0066) -- that no longer exists once guards are correctly tick-scaled too (FID-0066, chrlv.c chrlvFireWeaponRelated). fire_rate_authentic_off_hash is UNCHANGED and still exactly reproduces the pre-FID-0056/pre-FID-0066 legacy cadence (verified identical on both a git-archive build of b289b60, the pre-merge-train tip, and the current tree). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0066-npc-fire-rate.md and the P1a/Phase-B repair evidence for the full attribution (GE007_TRACE_GUARD_AUTOFIRE confirms chr=6 gate-fires 18x inside this tape's window at eff_rate=9). Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (e228f3a765adc3eb->8c1c6f13a440c0eb) for FID-0014: the faithful WAYMODE_MAGIC patrol semantics (default ON) freeze unseen patrolling/gopos guards and pad-warp them per retail chrTickBeams US 0x7F020EF0 / chrlvTravelTickMagic US 0x7F028600, so offscreen patroller positions (sim state) differ from the legacy creep-walk. GE007_NO_PATROL_MAGIC_FIX=1 reproduces the previous hash e228f3a765adc3eb byte-identically (verified on this tape). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0014-patrol-magic.md. fire_rate_authentic_off_hash likewise re-based a950d75eb42017f2->2c93c8d798811fe2 (= fix-ON default + GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0; the FID-0066 opt-out contract is unchanged).
